title: chonemorpha floccosa
intro: Chonemorpha floccosa, also known as the "tree with white flowers," is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. The plant is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. It is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating pain, as well as possessing antioxidant properties that may help protect against cell damage. In traditional herbal preparations, Chonemorpha floccosa is often used to make infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments.
